blocks:
Ayahs

لِإِيلَـٰفِ قُرَيْشٍ (۱)
[In gratitude] for solidarity among the Quraysh, (1)
إِۦلَـٰفِهِمْ رِحْلَةَ ٱلشِّتَآءِ وَٱلصَّيْفِ (۲)
their solidarity during winter and summer journeys, (2)
فَلْيَعْبُدُوا۟ رَبَّ هَـٰذَا ٱلْبَيْتِ (۳)
let them worship the Lord of this House, (3)
ٱلَّذِىٓ أَطْعَمَهُم مِّن جُوعٍ وَءَامَنَهُم مِّنْ خَوْفٍۭ (٤)
who has fed them [and saved them] from hunger, and secured them from fear. (4)

Key Points

Some exegetes consider this Surah a continuation of the previous Surah, citing narrations that deem it impermissible to separate the two in prayer, similar to the two Surahs of Ad-Duha and Al-Inshirah. Had Abrahah been victorious, the livelihood (ma'ash) of Quraysh would have been shattered, and Allah destroyed the People of the Elephant (Aṣḥāb al-Fīl) in order to preserve the livelihood of Quraysh. Riḥlah (رِحلة) means traversing a path upon a rāḥilah (riding animal) or mount. Quraysh had two commercial journeys annually: one in winter to the region of Yemen and one in summer to the region of Sham (Syria/Levant). In contrast to the custom of the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah), which valued bloodshed and war, the repetition of the word īlāf (familiarity/covenant) and ulfat (harmony) indicates Allah's grace and mercy upon Quraysh. Commercial journeys lead to the flourishing of exports and imports, resulting in improved living standards and cultural exchange. After the destruction of the People of the Elephant, people accorded special sanctity (ḥarīm) to the inhabitants of the Sanctuary (Ahl al-Ḥaram).

Messages

1- The defeat of enemies is a factor in your solidarity. «فجعلهم كعصف مأكول»

2- Unity and harmony among the segments of society are among the divine blessings. «لایلاف قریش»

3- Unless the economic and security issues of a society are resolved, people cannot be invited to worship. «فلیعبدوا... الّذى اطعمهم... و آمنهم»

4- One of the philosophies of worship is thanking the Benefactor (al-Mun'im). «فلیعبدوا ربّ هذا البیت»

5- Allah manages the affairs of Mecca and the Ka'bah. «ربّ هذا البیت»

6- Providing livelihood and security for society must be in the path of servitude and worship of God. «فلیعبدوا... الّذى اطعمهم... و آمنهم»

7- Hunger and the need for food are characteristics of humanity, and the provision of this need is a sign of Allah's grace and mercy. «اطعمهم من جوع»

8- Establishing security and removing factors of fear is a divine task. «آمنهم من خوف»

9- Travel for earning a livelihood is accepted in Islam. «رحلة الشتاء و الصیف»
